verdict.answer_first: A states the diagnosis immediately: "almost certainly a test-isolation problem"; B also starts similarly but adds more sections before the shortest path.
verdict.decisiveness: A commits to the core workflow: "Find the culprit pair" and "dropping to 2 workers"; B surveys more categories including timeout/load as a separate path.
verdict.fabrication: B invents questionable runner detail: "Go's default randomized order"; otherwise no repo/file-specific fabrication.
verdict.actionability: A is easier to act on now because it gives the minimal loop: reproduce, inspect failure, bisect to pair, isolate shared resource.
